title: Cephalomanes sumatranum
taxonomy: Cephalomanes sumatranum is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Cephalomanes and is classified as a type of filmy fern due to its delicate and translucent leaves. The Hymenophyllaceae family is a group of small to medium-sized ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Cephalomanes sumatranum is native to Sumatra, Indonesia, and other parts of Southeast Asia. As a member of the Hymenophyllaceae family, it has a unique characteristic of having leaf veins that are nearly invisible to the naked eye.
uses: Cephalomanes sumatranum is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, where its leaves are valued for their potential health benefits. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. Its leaves are also used to treat fever and other infections. In some parts of Southeast Asia, Cephalomanes sumatranum is used to reduce swelling and improve wound healing. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
